zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel William於大約1405年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托時，她的父親William Mark Macwilliam31歲，母親Agnes Solers27歲。她於1424年7月20日與Sir John Seymour I在英國英格蘭布里斯托結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1486年4月14日在英國維爾爾薩維爾納可 科華士享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Isabel William于大约1405年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托时，她的父亲William Mark Macwilliam31岁，母亲Agnes Solers27岁。她于1424年7月20日与Sir John Seymour I在英国英格兰布里斯托结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1486年4月14日在英国維爾爾薩維爾納可 科華士享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Memoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ciro, Francesco, Luciano, Natale, Nino.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a pet form of thepersonal name Guglielmo, Italian form of William .

variants.name: McElligott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Brendan, Seamus.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uileagóid, a patronymic from a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name William .

variants.name: McWilliam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain, Morag.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , also Anglicized as Williamson in Scotland. The name is thought to be mainly Scottish in Ulster, though there has been some confusion with McQuillan in County Down. In Connacht, McWilliam was a branch of Burke .

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: McWilliams
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , with excrescent English -s. In Ireland it sometimes represents a partial Gaelicization of Welsh Williams .

variants.name: Lemmo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Donato, Gino.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Lemmo, a pet form of Guglielmo, an equivalent of William . Compare Lembo .

variants.name: Wilk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wilkin, a pet form of William introduced to England and Scotland by Flemings and Picards after the Norman Conquest.
North German: variant of   Wilken  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Lembo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Domenic, Pietro, Alfio, Amelio, Antonio, Biagio, Dante, Giovani, Giovanni.
Italian (southern): from a hypercorrected form of Lemmo, a pet form of Guglièlmo, an equivalent of William .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabel William was born about 1405, in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, her father, William Mark Macwilliam, was 31 and her mother, Agnes Solers, was 27. She married Sir John Seymour I on 20 July 1424, in Bristol, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 14 April 1486, in Savernake Forest, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel William est née environ 1405, à Bristol, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, William Mark Macwilliam, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Agnes Solers, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Seymour I le 20 juillet 1424, à Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 14 avril 1486, à Savernake Forest, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabel William um 1405 in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Mark Macwilliam 31 und ihre Mutter Agnes Solers 27. Sie hat am 20. Juli 1424 in Bristol, England, United Kingdom Sir John Seymour I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 14. it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel William è nata il circa 1405 nel Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, William Mark Macwilliam, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Agnes Solers, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Seymour I il 20 luglio 1424, nel Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 14 aprile 1486, nel Savernake Forest, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Williamが約1405年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストルで生まれた時，父William Mark Macwilliamは31歳，母Agnes Solersは27歳であった。1424年7月20日にイギリスイギリスブリストルでSir John Seymour Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1486年4月14日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサバーナク フォーエストで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel William(이)가 대략 1405년 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Mark Macwilliam(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Solers(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1424년 7월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨에서 Sir John Seymour I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1486년 4월 14일 영국 윌츠히르 새버나크 포레스트에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel William nasceu aproximadamente 1405, em Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Mark Macwilliam, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Solers, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Seymour I em 20 de julho de 1424, em Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 14 de abril de 1486, em Savernake Forest, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabel William родилась около 1405 г. в местности бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Mark Macwilliam было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes Solers, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Seymour I 20 июля 1424 г. в местности бристол, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 14 апреля 1486 г. в местности савирнеик форест, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Isabel William nació aproximadamente 1405, en Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Mark Macwilliam, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Agnes Solers, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Sir John Seymour I el 20 de julio de 1424, en Bristol, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 14 de abril de 1486, en Savernake Forest, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años.
